data.title: Directory size monitor: advisory session-start hook with auto-ticketing
data.description:
## Context
Directories in DSO-managed repositories accumulate files over time, making navigation harder and increasing cognitive load. Today there is no mechanism to detect when a directory has grown unwieldy — tests/scripts currently has 351 files, tests/hooks has 157, and plugins/dso/scripts has 156. Developers and agents don't notice the gradual growth until a directory is deeply problematic. An advisory session-start hook that detects oversized directories and auto-creates backlog epic tickets ensures refactoring work is captured systematically without disrupting active workflows.

## Success Criteria
- A session-start hook scans all git-tracked files, counts files per directory, and identifies directories exceeding the configured threshold
- Directories matching .gitignore entries (via git ls-files), .git/, and a configurable exclusion list in dso-config.conf are excluded from scanning
- When a directory exceeds the threshold and no open ticket tagged dir:<path> exists, a P4 epic is auto-created with a descriptive title and the dir:<path> tag
- No duplicate tickets are created for the same directory — deduplication checks open tickets only, allowing re-flagging after a previous ticket is closed
- The hook prints an advisory summary of flagged directories to stderr without blocking session startup
- Threshold and exclusion list are configurable via dso-config.conf keys (directory_monitor.file_threshold default 30, directory_monitor.exclude_dirs default .git)

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
Session-start hook script wired into the existing session-start dispatcher. Uses git ls-files (which naturally excludes .gitignore entries) piped through dirname/sort/uniq -c to count files per directory. Applies configurable exclusion list from dso-config.conf. For each directory over threshold, queries ticket system for open tickets tagged dir:<normalized-path>. Creates P4 epic if none found. Prints advisory summary to stderr.

## Scenario Analysis
- Threshold of 0 or 1 floods ticket system (medium, configuration): Setting the threshold to 0 or 1 causes every directory to trigger a ticket, creating dozens of epics in one session. Mitigation: enforce a minimum threshold or document the valid range.
- Exclude path format mismatch (medium, configuration): Path normalization differences between the exclusion list and the scanner could cause exclusions to silently fail. Mitigation: normalize paths before comparison.
